roles.name: Physical Security and Site Access Manager

issues.name: NIS2 critical-entity classification of Voltara's grid-connected generation portfolio
issues.description: Voltara's onshore wind, solar and hydro plants fall under the NIS2 Directive's energy sector as essential entities, imposing stricter incident-reporting and management-accountability obligations.

issues.name: EU electricity market price volatility eroding merchant revenue predictability
issues.description: Wholesale day-ahead price swings on EPEX SPOT undermine the revenue stability of Voltara's merchant solar and wind assets sold outside power-purchase agreements.

issues.name: Phase-out of feed-in tariffs for legacy French wind farms
issues.description: The expiry of 15-year guaranteed feed-in tariffs on several early Voltara wind sites forces a transition to contract-for-difference or merchant exposure with lower margins.

issues.name: Concentration of SCADA expertise in a small in-house OT team
issues.description: Operational-technology monitoring of remote turbines and inverters depends on a handful of specialised engineers, creating a key-person dependency risk.

issues.name: Aging hydroelectric concession assets on the Rhone tributaries
issues.description: Voltara operates run-of-river hydro plants whose mechanical and civil works are nearing end of design life and require costly refurbishment.

issues.name: Supply-chain dependence on non-EU photovoltaic module manufacturers
issues.description: Most solar panels for new Voltara installations are sourced from Asian manufacturers, exposing projects to trade restrictions, tariffs and forced-labour due-diligence scrutiny.

issues.name: Rising cyber-threat targeting of European renewable-energy operators
issues.description: Nation-state and ransomware actors increasingly target distributed energy resources and remote-monitoring platforms across the European power sector.

issues.name: GDPR obligations for smart-metering and prosumer customer data
issues.description: Voltara's consumer-facing solar leasing and metering services process personal consumption data subject to GDPR lawfulness, retention and access requirements.

issues.name: Local community opposition to new onshore wind permitting in rural France
issues.description: Planned wind developments face organised local objection and protracted permitting that delay project timelines and raise development costs.

issues.name: Skilled wind-technician labour shortage across the European market
issues.description: A scarce pool of certified turbine maintenance technicians inflates wages and lengthens recruitment for Voltara's growing fleet.

issues.name: Fragmented IT-OT network segmentation across acquired sites
issues.description: Sites acquired through prior expansions run heterogeneous network architectures with inconsistent IT and OT segmentation, complicating security baselining.

issues.name: Grid-connection queue congestion delaying new project commissioning
issues.description: Lengthy transmission-operator interconnection queues postpone the revenue start date of Voltara's pipeline solar and wind projects.

issues.name: Climate change altering long-term wind and hydrological resource patterns
issues.description: Shifting precipitation and wind regimes introduce uncertainty into Voltara's long-term energy-yield forecasts and asset siting decisions.

issues.name: Dependence on a single ERP platform for finance and asset management
issues.description: Voltara's core business processes run on one ERP system whose outage or misconfiguration would disrupt accounting, procurement and maintenance planning.

issues.name: EU Taxonomy and CSRD sustainability-reporting compliance burden
issues.description: Voltara must align disclosures with the EU Taxonomy and Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, requiring new data collection and assurance processes.

issues.name: Remote-site physical security exposure of unmanned substations
issues.description: Geographically dispersed unmanned substations and inverter stations are vulnerable to vandalism, copper theft and unauthorised physical access.

issues.name: Increasing investor and lender ESG scrutiny of governance maturity
issues.description: Project-finance lenders and equity investors condition funding on demonstrable cybersecurity, governance and risk-management maturity at Voltara.

issues.name: Knowledge loss from impending retirement of founding engineering staff
issues.description: Several long-tenured engineers who designed early hydro and wind assets are approaching retirement, risking undocumented operational knowledge.

issues.name: Reliance on third-party O&M contractors for turbine maintenance
issues.description: Outsourced original-equipment-manufacturer maintenance contracts give external parties privileged remote and physical access to critical generation assets.

issues.name: Inflationary pressure on construction and commodity costs for new builds
issues.description: Rising prices for steel, copper, concrete and balance-of-plant components increase the capital cost of Voltara's development pipeline.

issues.name: Inadequate centralised asset inventory across the generation fleet
issues.description: Voltara lacks a single authoritative inventory of OT devices, firmware versions and network endpoints across all sites, hampering vulnerability management.

issues.name: Geopolitical instability affecting rare-earth and battery raw-material supply
issues.description: Concentration of rare-earth magnets and battery materials in a few exporting countries exposes Voltara's turbine and storage procurement to geopolitical disruption.

issues.name: Cross-border data-transfer constraints for cloud-hosted monitoring
issues.description: Use of cloud monitoring services with infrastructure outside the EEA raises GDPR international-transfer compliance questions for operational and personal data.

issues.name: Internal change-management discipline lagging rapid fleet expansion
issues.description: The pace of acquisitions and new commissioning outstrips Voltara's formal change-control and documentation processes, increasing configuration risk.

issues.name: Public and regulatory pressure to accelerate decarbonisation timelines
issues.description: National and EU climate targets create demand and political support for Voltara to expand renewable capacity faster than current capabilities allow.

issues.name: Dependence on regional transmission operator dispatch and curtailment decisions
issues.description: Voltara's revenue is exposed to grid-operator curtailment instructions during oversupply or congestion events on the French and neighbouring grids.

issues.name: Weak security culture and phishing susceptibility among field staff
issues.description: Distributed field teams with limited security awareness training are a frequent entry point for phishing and credential-compromise attempts.

issues.name: Emergence of grid-scale battery storage reshaping the business model
issues.description: The rapid maturation of battery storage and hybrid plants forces Voltara to develop new technical and commercial competencies to stay competitive.

issues.name: Insurance market hardening for renewable-asset and cyber coverage
issues.description: Rising premiums and tightening conditions for natural-catastrophe and cyber insurance increase Voltara's risk-transfer costs and retained exposure.

issues.name: Limited formal segregation of duties in procurement workflows
issues.description: Lean back-office staffing leads to overlapping responsibilities in purchasing and payment approval, weakening fraud and error controls.

issues.name: French ANSSI and CRE sector-specific cybersecurity expectations
issues.description: National authorities impose energy-sector cybersecurity and resilience expectations that Voltara must meet alongside EU-wide NIS2 obligations.

issues.name: Extreme-weather damage to dispersed solar and wind installations
issues.description: More frequent storms, hail, heatwaves and flooding threaten physical damage and downtime across Voltara's geographically spread assets.

issues.name: Reliance on legacy unpatched firmware in deployed inverters
issues.description: Older photovoltaic inverters in the fleet run vendor firmware that is difficult to patch, leaving known vulnerabilities unresolved.

issues.name: Workforce diversity and inclusion gaps in a male-dominated technical sector
issues.description: Voltara struggles to attract and retain a diverse technical workforce, affecting talent reach, reputation and innovation capacity.

issues.name: Mergers-and-acquisitions integration risk from portfolio consolidation
issues.description: Industry consolidation drives Voltara to acquire smaller developers, bringing integration challenges across systems, controls and cultures.

issues.name: Dependence on satellite and cellular links for remote-site connectivity
issues.description: Generation sites in low-coverage rural areas rely on cellular and satellite backhaul whose outages interrupt monitoring and control.

issues.name: Evolving EU Cyber Resilience Act obligations for connected equipment
issues.description: The Cyber Resilience Act will impose security requirements on the connected devices and components Voltara procures and integrates into its plants.

issues.name: Hydro concession renewal uncertainty under French public-tender reform
issues.description: Pending reform of French hydropower concession awards creates uncertainty over Voltara's ability to retain operating rights on existing dams.

issues.name: Insufficient documented business-continuity and disaster-recovery plans
issues.description: Voltara's continuity and recovery procedures for its monitoring centre and core systems are incomplete and rarely exercised.

issues.name: Reputational sensitivity to environmental incidents at hydro sites
issues.description: Fish-passage, sediment and water-level incidents at run-of-river plants attract regulatory and public scrutiny that can damage Voltara's standing.

issues.name: Competition for prime land and grid-connection sites with rival developers
issues.description: Intense competition for well-sited land and scarce grid capacity drives up acquisition costs and squeezes Voltara's project returns.

issues.name: Heavy reliance on contractor and intern staffing during peak seasons
issues.description: Seasonal use of temporary contractors and interns for construction and maintenance complicates access management and consistent security practices.

issues.name: Volatility in green-certificate and guarantee-of-origin markets
issues.description: Fluctuating prices for guarantees of origin and green certificates affect a secondary revenue stream for Voltara's generation output.

issues.name: Centralisation of all operations in a single Lyon monitoring centre
issues.description: Concentrating fleet supervision and control in one Lyon operations centre creates a single point of failure for monitoring continuity.

issues.name: Rising interest rates increasing the cost of project-finance debt
issues.description: Higher borrowing costs reduce the financial viability of Voltara's capital-intensive renewable development projects.

issues.name: Shadow IT and unsanctioned SaaS adoption by business units
issues.description: Teams adopt unapproved cloud tools to move quickly, creating ungoverned data flows and gaps in Voltara's security oversight.

issues.name: Growing electrification demand boosting long-term power offtake interest
issues.description: Industrial electrification and corporate decarbonisation pledges expand demand for long-term renewable power-purchase agreements with Voltara.

issues.name: Dependence on a limited number of grid-code-compliant turbine vendors
issues.description: A narrow set of turbine suppliers meeting evolving grid-code requirements concentrates Voltara's procurement and warranty risk.

issues.name: Pressure to demonstrate ISO 27001 certification to corporate offtakers
issues.description: Large corporate power buyers increasingly require certified information-security management as a condition of signing offtake contracts with Voltara.

issues.name: Inconsistent identity and access management across IT and OT domains
issues.description: Separate, loosely governed account systems for office IT and plant OT lead to orphaned accounts and inconsistent access controls.

issues.name: Regional drought reducing hydro generation availability
issues.description: Prolonged low-water periods on French rivers cut the generation output and revenue of Voltara's run-of-river hydro fleet.

issues.name: Demand for real-time renewable-data transparency from grid stakeholders
issues.description: Transmission operators and aggregators expect timely, accurate generation data, pressuring Voltara's telemetry and data-quality processes.

issues.name: Limited maturity of supplier security-risk assessment processes
issues.description: Voltara has no systematic process to assess and monitor the security posture of its many equipment and service suppliers.

issues.name: Talent competition from larger utilities and tech firms for data skills
issues.description: Bigger energy and technology companies outbid Voltara for data-engineering and cybersecurity talent needed to modernise operations.

issues.name: Increasing frequency of regulatory audits and energy-sector inspections
issues.description: Authorities intensify audits of energy operators' security and resilience controls, raising Voltara's compliance evidencing workload.

issues.name: Internal pressure to deliver digital transformation with constrained budgets
issues.description: Leadership demands rapid digitalisation of monitoring and analytics while IT and security budgets remain tightly capped.

stakeholders.name: CNIL (Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés)
stakeholders.description: French data protection authority enforcing GDPR. Oversees lawful processing of employee, customer and SCADA-adjacent personal data; can audit, issue formal notices and levy fines for breaches.

stakeholders.name: ANSSI (Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information)
stakeholders.description: French national cybersecurity agency. As an energy operator, Voltara falls under NIS2 / OIV-OSE obligations; ANSSI sets security rules, runs inspections and is the CSIRT for incident notification.

stakeholders.name: Commission de Régulation de l'Énergie (CRE)
stakeholders.description: French energy market regulator. Governs grid access, network tariffs and market conduct; expects Voltara to maintain availability and integrity of trading and dispatch systems.

stakeholders.name: RTE Réseau de Transport d'Électricité
stakeholders.description: French transmission system operator. Grid balancing partner that connects Voltara's wind, solar and hydro plants; relies on accurate, timely telemetry and curtailment signals.

stakeholders.name: Enedis
stakeholders.description: French distribution network operator. Manages low/medium-voltage connection of distributed solar and small hydro assets; exchanges metering and injection data with Voltara.

stakeholders.name: European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA)
stakeholders.description: EU cybersecurity body shaping NIS2 implementation guidance and sector threat landscapes for energy. Provides reference frameworks Voltara aligns its ISMS controls against.

stakeholders.name: Bureau Veritas Certification France
stakeholders.description: Accredited certification body conducting Voltara's ISO 27001 surveillance and recertification audits. Independent assurance over the ISMS scope, SoA and corrective actions.

stakeholders.name: AFNOR Certification
stakeholders.description: Certification and standards body engaged for ISO 9001/14001 alignment and as an alternate auditor pool. Influences integrated management system documentation expectations.

stakeholders.name: EDF Renouvelables
stakeholders.description: Co-investment and offtake partner on two onshore wind portfolios. Shares operational data and demands contractual security and confidentiality clauses on joint asset platforms.

stakeholders.name: Engie Green
stakeholders.description: Industrial partner in a shared solar development pipeline. Joint venture exchanges project finance models, land data and SCADA integration interfaces requiring secure data rooms.

stakeholders.name: Voltaeon Trading SAS
stakeholders.description: Power-purchase and energy-trading counterparty buying Voltara's renewable output via PPAs and the day-ahead market. Sensitive to settlement data integrity and platform uptime.

stakeholders.name: Métropole de Lyon
stakeholders.description: Local authority hosting Voltara's headquarters and several rooftop-solar municipal contracts. Stakeholder in local permitting, public reporting and community energy commitments.

stakeholders.name: Préfecture du Rhône
stakeholders.description: Regional state representative granting ICPE operating authorizations and overseeing Seveso-adjacent and grid-security obligations for installations in the Rhône département.

stakeholders.name: Direction régionale de l'environnement (DREAL Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes)
stakeholders.description: Regional environmental authority. Inspects hydro and wind sites for environmental compliance, water-use permits and protected-species impact monitoring.

stakeholders.name: France Nature Environnement Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
stakeholders.description: Environmental NGO that scrutinizes wind and hydro projects for biodiversity and landscape impact. Can challenge permits and shapes public acceptance and reputation.

stakeholders.name: Ligue pour la Protection des Oiseaux (LPO)
stakeholders.description: Bird-protection NGO concerned with avian collision risk at onshore wind farms. Negotiates curtailment-for-wildlife agreements and monitors mitigation commitments.

stakeholders.name: AXA Corporate Solutions
stakeholders.description: Primary property and business-interruption insurer for Voltara's generation assets. Requires evidence of cyber and physical risk controls to price and renew coverage.

stakeholders.name: Hiscox Cyber Insurance
stakeholders.description: Cyber-risk insurer underwriting Voltara's breach, ransomware and OT-incident cover. Conducts annual security questionnaires and may set control prerequisites for claims.

stakeholders.name: Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy
stakeholders.description: Wind-turbine OEM and maintenance partner with remote-monitoring access to turbine controllers. Critical OT supply-chain dependency under NIS2 third-party scrutiny.

stakeholders.name: Vestas Wind Systems
stakeholders.description: Turbine manufacturer and service provider for part of the onshore fleet. Provides firmware updates and remote diagnostics, creating a managed remote-access trust boundary.

stakeholders.name: Schneider Electric
stakeholders.description: SCADA and electrical-equipment supplier underpinning plant control systems. Key vendor for ICS patching, hardening guidance and substation automation security.

stakeholders.name: Microsoft Ireland Operations (Azure)
stakeholders.description: Cloud infrastructure provider hosting Voltara's corporate IT and analytics workloads. GDPR data-processor with EU data-residency commitments and shared-responsibility security model.

stakeholders.name: OVHcloud
stakeholders.description: French sovereign-cloud provider hosting backups and selected regulated workloads. Chosen for EU sovereignty and SecNumCloud-aligned hosting under NIS2 supply-chain rules.

stakeholders.name: Orange Business Services
stakeholders.description: Telecom and connectivity supplier linking remote substations and wind/solar sites. Provides MPLS, SD-WAN and field connectivity critical to OT availability.

stakeholders.name: Capgemini
stakeholders.description: IT systems integrator and managed-services partner for ERP and data-platform projects. Processes internal data and holds privileged access requiring contractual security controls.

stakeholders.name: Deloitte France
stakeholders.description: External auditor and advisory firm performing financial and select internal-control reviews. Relies on integrity of Voltara's reporting and ISMS evidence.

stakeholders.name: Wavestone
stakeholders.description: Cybersecurity consultancy engaged for penetration testing, NIS2 gap analysis and incident-response retainer. Trusted third party handling sensitive vulnerability findings.

stakeholders.name: Banque Publique d'Investissement (Bpifrance)
stakeholders.description: Public financing partner backing renewable project debt. Imposes ESG, governance and operational-resilience reporting covenants on funded portfolios.

stakeholders.name: Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank
stakeholders.description: Project-finance lender on hydro and solar assets. Lender-of-record requiring covenant compliance, insurance evidence and operational continuity assurances.

stakeholders.name: European Investment Bank (EIB)
stakeholders.description: Supranational lender co-financing grid-connected solar expansion. Requires climate-impact, anti-fraud and information-governance reporting throughout the loan tenor.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Energy Works Council (Comité Social et Économique)
stakeholders.description: Employee representative body consulted on workforce data processing, remote-work policies and any monitoring or surveillance impacting staff privacy under French labour law.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Field Operations and Maintenance Technicians
stakeholders.description: On-site staff operating and maintaining wind, solar and hydro plants. Daily users of OT/SCADA interfaces; primary stakeholders for OT access control and safety-security balance.

stakeholders.name: Voltara SOC and IT Security Team
stakeholders.description: Internal security operations team running monitoring, vulnerability management and incident response across IT and OT. Owns and operates much of the ISMS control set.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Data Protection Officer
stakeholders.description: Designated DPO accountable for GDPR compliance, RoPA maintenance, DPIAs and acting as the contact point with the CNIL and data subjects.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Chief Information Security Officer (Élodie Marchand)
stakeholders.description: Executive owner of the ISMS, NIS2 readiness and risk-acceptance authority. Reports security posture to the executive committee and the board.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Chief Executive Officer (Henri Delacroix)
stakeholders.description: Top management providing ISMS leadership commitment, approving the security policy and risk appetite, and accountable for regulatory compliance to the board.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Board of Directors
stakeholders.description: Governing body with ultimate oversight of enterprise risk, NIS2 management accountability and approval of major security investments and risk acceptances.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Legal and Compliance Department
stakeholders.description: Internal function managing regulatory mapping (GDPR, NIS2, energy law), contracts, supplier clauses and breach-notification obligations across the organization.

stakeholders.name: Voltara OT/SCADA Engineering Team
stakeholders.description: Control-systems engineers designing and maintaining plant automation. Stakeholders in OT segmentation, secure remote access and ICS patching strategy.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Procurement and Vendor Management
stakeholders.description: Internal team owning third-party onboarding, supplier risk assessments and contractual security requirements central to NIS2 supply-chain due diligence.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Internal Audit Function
stakeholders.description: Independent assurance team auditing ISMS effectiveness, control operation and management-system conformity ahead of external certification audits.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Human Resources Department
stakeholders.description: Owner of employee personal data, onboarding/offboarding access lifecycle, security awareness training delivery and disciplinary follow-up on policy breaches.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Energy Trading Desk
stakeholders.description: Internal team executing market sales and PPAs. High-sensitivity users dependent on the integrity and availability of trading and settlement platforms.

stakeholders.name: Coopérative Citoyenne Énergies du Rhône
stakeholders.description: Community-energy cooperative co-owning a local solar park with residents. Stakeholder in transparent reporting, local participation and reputational trust.

stakeholders.name: Agence de l'Eau Rhône-Méditerranée-Corse
stakeholders.description: Water authority regulating Voltara's run-of-river hydro abstraction and ecological-flow obligations. Monitors compliance with water-management permits.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Solar Park Neighbouring Landowners Association
stakeholders.description: Group of landowners hosting or bordering solar and wind installations. Stakeholders in lease compliance, noise, glare and land-use grievance handling.

stakeholders.name: Le Monde Énergie Desk
stakeholders.description: National media outlet covering the energy transition. Reputational stakeholder whose reporting on incidents, outages or breaches affects public and investor perception.

stakeholders.name: Mediapart Investigations
stakeholders.description: Investigative media organization that scrutinizes governance, environmental and data-handling practices. Heightens transparency and breach-disclosure reputational risk.

stakeholders.name: Syndicat des Énergies Renouvelables (SER)
stakeholders.description: French renewable-energy trade association. Voltara participates in policy advocacy, sector best-practice sharing and coordinated NIS2 implementation guidance.

stakeholders.name: Cybermalveillance.gouv.fr
stakeholders.description: French national cyber-incident assistance platform. Reference and support resource for incident response and victim guidance, complementing ANSSI reporting channels.

stakeholders.name: Voltara Grand Wind Cluster Host Municipality (Commune de Saint-Étienne-du-Bois)
stakeholders.description: Rural municipality hosting a flagship onshore wind cluster. Local-authority stakeholder in permits, local taxation, community relations and emergency coordination.

stakeholders.name: Trust Center Customers and Corporate PPA Offtakers
stakeholders.description: Industrial electricity buyers who request Voltara's security posture, certifications and subprocessor disclosures via the trust center before signing long-term contracts.

stakeholders.name: Solaria Components Iberia S.L.
stakeholders.description: Photovoltaic-module and inverter supplier for solar farms. Hardware supply-chain dependency where firmware integrity and counterfeit-component risk are assessed.

stakeholders.name: HydroMech Alpine Services GmbH
stakeholders.description: Specialist hydro-turbine and penstock maintenance contractor with periodic on-site and remote access to hydro plant control systems.

stakeholders.name: GreenAudit Verification BV
stakeholders.description: Independent ESG and greenhouse-gas verification body assuring Voltara's sustainability disclosures, which are increasingly tied to lender and investor commitments.

stakeholders.name: Caisse des Dépôts et Consignations
stakeholders.description: Public institutional investor holding an equity stake in Voltara's infrastructure fund. Stakeholder in long-term governance, resilience and responsible-investment standards.

objectives.name: Reduce mean time to detect security incidents in the SOC
objectives.description: Lower the average time from a malicious event occurring to its detection by the security operations centre, measured across all confirmed incidents, targeting a maximum mean time-to-detect of 4 hours by year-end.

objectives.name: Patch critical vulnerabilities on internet-facing assets within 14 days
objectives.description: Remediate all CVSS 9.0+ vulnerabilities identified on externally exposed systems (VPN gateways, supplier portals, SCADA jump hosts) within 14 calendar days of disclosure, tracked via the vulnerability management platform.

objectives.name: Achieve ISO 27001:2022 certification audit readiness across the ISMS scope
objectives.description: Reach 95% closure of Statement of Applicability control gaps ahead of the external Stage 2 audit covering the Lyon headquarters and managed wind, solar and hydro sites.

objectives.name: Complete NIS2 incident notification to ANSSI within 24 hours of awareness
objectives.description: Submit the early warning for any significant incident affecting essential services to the French national competent authority within the mandatory 24-hour window in 100% of qualifying cases.

objectives.name: Resolve GDPR data subject access requests within the statutory one-month deadline
objectives.description: Fulfil verified data subject access, erasure and rectification requests from employees, landowners and consumers within 30 calendar days, targeting a 100% on-time completion rate.

objectives.name: Increase phishing simulation resilience among field and corporate staff
objectives.description: Reduce the click-through rate on quarterly simulated phishing campaigns to below 4% across the workforce, including remote turbine technicians and substation operators.

objectives.name: Maintain SCADA network segmentation integrity for OT environments
objectives.description: Ensure 100% of wind, solar and hydro control networks remain logically isolated from corporate IT through audited firewall rule reviews and verified unidirectional gateways at each site.

objectives.name: Reduce mean time to recover critical generation control systems after an incident
objectives.description: Restore distributed control systems and remote terminal units to operational state within a 6-hour recovery time objective following a cyber or hardware disruption.

objectives.name: Achieve full multi-factor authentication coverage for privileged accounts
objectives.description: Enforce phishing-resistant MFA on 100% of administrative, domain, OT engineering and remote-access privileged accounts across all subsidiaries.

objectives.name: Conduct quarterly access recertification for high-risk applications
objectives.description: Perform 4 formal access reviews per year for the energy trading platform, SAP, the SCADA historian and the GDPR records-of-processing repository, with documented manager sign-off.

objectives.name: Reduce overdue corrective actions from internal ISMS audits
objectives.description: Drive the backlog of past-due corrective and preventive actions arising from internal audits and management reviews to fewer than 5 open items at any time.

objectives.name: Increase encryption coverage of laptops and mobile devices at rest
objectives.description: Maintain full-disk encryption on 100% of company laptops, tablets and field engineering devices, verified through MDM compliance reporting.

objectives.name: Improve backup restoration success rate for business-critical systems
objectives.description: Achieve a 99% successful restore rate in quarterly recovery tests of ERP, GIS, SCADA historian and document management backups.

objectives.name: Reduce the percentage of endpoints missing EDR agent coverage
objectives.description: Keep the share of corporate and OT-adjacent endpoints without an active endpoint detection and response agent below 1%, measured weekly.

objectives.name: Complete annual security awareness training for all personnel
objectives.description: Achieve 98% completion of the mandatory annual information security and data protection e-learning across employees, contractors and seasonal maintenance crews.

objectives.name: Decrease the average age of unremediated medium-severity vulnerabilities
objectives.description: Reduce the mean open age of CVSS 4.0 to 6.9 vulnerabilities across the IT estate to under 60 days through prioritised remediation sprints.

objectives.name: Achieve timely revocation of access for departing employees and contractors
objectives.description: Deprovision all system, building and remote-access credentials within 4 hours of an employee or contractor offboarding being confirmed by HR.

objectives.name: Increase supplier security assessment coverage for critical third parties
objectives.description: Complete documented security and NIS2 supply-chain risk assessments for 100% of critical suppliers including turbine OEMs, SCADA vendors and cloud providers before contract renewal.

objectives.name: Reduce unplanned downtime of the energy management system due to cyber events
objectives.description: Limit cumulative annual unplanned downtime of the central energy management and dispatch platform attributable to security incidents to under 8 hours.

objectives.name: Improve log coverage and central ingestion across in-scope systems
objectives.description: Ensure 95% of in-scope IT and OT assets forward security-relevant logs to the central SIEM with verified retention of at least 12 months.

objectives.name: Reduce false positive rate of SIEM detection rules
objectives.description: Tune correlation rules to bring the proportion of triaged alerts closed as false positives below 30%, improving analyst efficiency in the SOC.

objectives.name: Achieve on-time review of all ISMS policies and procedures
objectives.description: Review and re-approve 100% of information security policies, OT security standards and data protection procedures within their scheduled annual cycle.

objectives.name: Increase coverage of data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
objectives.description: Complete DPIAs for 100% of new or changed processing activities classified as high-risk under GDPR, such as employee monitoring and smart-metering analytics.

objectives.name: Reduce the number of shadow IT cloud applications in use
objectives.description: Cut the count of unsanctioned SaaS applications discovered by the CASB to fewer than 10 across the organisation through governance and approved alternatives.

objectives.name: Improve mean time to contain confirmed security incidents
objectives.description: Achieve isolation or containment of confirmed incidents within an average of 2 hours of detection, measured across the incident response register.

objectives.name: Increase penetration test remediation closure within agreed timelines
objectives.description: Remediate 90% of high and critical findings from annual penetration tests of the trading platform, customer portal and OT gateways within 90 days.

objectives.name: Reduce privileged session activity without recording or approval
objectives.description: Ensure that fewer than 2% of privileged remote-access sessions to OT and crown-jewel systems occur without recording and just-in-time approval through the PAM solution.

objectives.name: Achieve business continuity exercise participation across critical functions
objectives.description: Run at least 3 business continuity and crisis simulation exercises per year covering generation dispatch, trading and corporate IT, with documented lessons learned.

objectives.name: Reduce the proportion of assets lacking a designated owner in the CMDB
objectives.description: Bring the share of information and OT assets without an accountable owner recorded in the configuration management database below 3%.

objectives.name: Improve timeliness of security patch deployment to OT field devices
objectives.description: Deploy validated firmware and security patches to programmable logic controllers and RTUs at substations and wind farms within a 45-day maintenance-window target.

objectives.name: Increase data classification labelling coverage on corporate documents
objectives.description: Apply approved sensitivity labels to 85% of newly created documents in the collaboration platform to support handling and DLP enforcement.

objectives.name: Reduce email-borne malware reaching user inboxes
objectives.description: Keep the share of malicious emails bypassing the secure email gateway and reaching inboxes below 0.5%, measured against total inbound malicious volume.

objectives.name: Achieve full inventory accuracy of internet-exposed assets
objectives.description: Reconcile the external attack surface management findings against the asset register so that 100% of internet-facing assets are known and authorised, with zero unexpected exposures.

objectives.name: Reduce the number of repeat audit findings across consecutive cycles
objectives.description: Eliminate recurrence of previously closed findings, targeting zero repeat nonconformities between successive internal and external ISMS audits.

objectives.name: Improve detection coverage mapped to MITRE ATT&CK techniques
objectives.description: Increase the share of relevant ATT&CK techniques covered by validated detection rules to 70%, with emphasis on ICS-targeted tactics affecting generation control.

objectives.name: Reduce vendor remote-access connections without time-bound authorisation
objectives.description: Drive to zero the number of standing, always-on remote-access tunnels from maintenance vendors into OT environments, replacing them with brokered time-limited sessions.

objectives.name: Increase encryption of data in transit for inter-site communications
objectives.description: Ensure 100% of WAN links between Lyon headquarters, control centres and generation sites carry traffic over authenticated, encrypted tunnels.

objectives.name: Achieve timely closure of GDPR data breach internal investigations
objectives.description: Complete root-cause investigations and remediation decisions for confirmed personal data breaches within 10 working days of detection.

objectives.name: Reduce service account credentials older than the rotation policy
objectives.description: Keep the proportion of service and machine accounts with secrets exceeding the 180-day rotation policy below 5%, tracked via the secrets management vault.

objectives.name: Improve security requirements coverage in procurement contracts
objectives.description: Embed approved information security and NIS2 clauses in 100% of new contracts with ICT service providers and OT equipment suppliers.

objectives.name: Reduce time to provision least-privilege access for new joiners
objectives.description: Grant role-based, least-privilege access to required systems for new employees within 2 working days of their start date via the IAM workflow.

objectives.name: Increase coverage of tabletop incident response exercises by scenario type
objectives.description: Conduct at least 4 distinct tabletop exercises per year covering ransomware, OT compromise, data breach and supply-chain attack scenarios.

objectives.name: Reduce dwell time of unauthorised access on the network
objectives.description: Lower the average dwell time of any detected unauthorised presence to under 1 day through improved monitoring and threat hunting.

objectives.name: Achieve completion of role-based security training for OT engineers
objectives.description: Ensure 100% of control-system and substation engineers complete specialised OT cybersecurity training annually, including secure remote maintenance practices.

objectives.name: Reduce the share of firewall rules without documented business justification
objectives.description: Bring the proportion of firewall rules lacking a recorded owner and justification below 5% through semi-annual rule base reviews.

objectives.name: Improve timeliness of management review of ISMS performance
objectives.description: Hold at least 2 documented top-management ISMS reviews per year covering objectives, incidents, audit results and resource needs.

objectives.name: Reduce reliance on legacy unsupported operating systems in production
objectives.description: Decommission or compensate for legacy unsupported operating systems, reducing the count of such systems in production to fewer than 8 by year-end.

objectives.name: Increase verified disaster recovery failover capability for the data centre
objectives.description: Demonstrate successful failover of 100% of tier-1 services to the secondary data centre during the annual disaster recovery test within the recovery objectives.

objectives.name: Reduce the percentage of high-risk findings overdue past their due date
objectives.description: Keep the share of high and critical risk treatment actions overdue beyond their committed due date below 10% in the risk register.

objectives.name: Improve mobile device compliance with security baseline policies
objectives.description: Achieve 97% of enrolled mobile devices compliant with passcode, encryption, OS-version and jailbreak-detection baselines reported by the MDM.

objectives.name: Reduce time to publish security advisories to affected internal teams
objectives.description: Disseminate actionable internal advisories for newly disclosed critical vulnerabilities affecting Voltara technologies within 8 hours of validation.

objectives.name: Achieve coverage of network access control for wired and wireless ports
objectives.description: Enforce 802.1X or equivalent network access control on 95% of corporate wired ports and wireless access points to block unauthorised devices.

objectives.name: Reduce average resolution time for security exceptions and risk acceptances
objectives.description: Lower the mean time to formally review and decide on requested security policy exceptions to under 15 days, with documented compensating controls.

objectives.name: Increase data retention policy compliance across personal data stores
objectives.description: Ensure 90% of identified personal data repositories enforce automated retention and deletion aligned with the GDPR data retention schedule.

objectives.name: Reduce the number of critical alarms unacknowledged in OT monitoring
objectives.description: Keep the count of critical OT cybersecurity monitoring alarms unacknowledged beyond 30 minutes to fewer than 2 per month across all control centres.

objectives.name: Improve third-party penetration coverage of the customer self-service portal
objectives.description: Commission at least 2 independent security assessments per year of the consumer and landowner self-service portal, covering authentication and data exposure.

activities.name: Onshore Wind Farm Power Generation
activities.description: Operation and active power production from Voltara's onshore wind portfolio across France, Germany and Spain, including turbine start-up/shutdown sequencing, yaw and pitch optimisation, and curtailment management in response to grid signals.

activities.name: Utility-Scale Solar PV Generation
activities.description: Day-to-day electricity production from ground-mounted photovoltaic plants, covering inverter operation, string-level performance monitoring, soiling assessment and tracker positioning to maximise yield across daylight hours.

activities.name: Run-of-River Hydroelectric Generation
activities.description: Continuous power production from small and medium run-of-river hydro stations, including flow-rate management, intake screen control, turbine governor regulation and compliance with environmental minimum-flow obligations.

activities.name: Real-Time Generation Dispatch & Balancing
activities.description: Coordination of fleet-wide active power output against grid frequency and balancing-market obligations, issuing dispatch instructions to assets and managing imbalance exposure through the central control room.

activities.name: Day-Ahead Market Bidding
activities.description: Formulation and submission of hourly price-volume bids into EPEX SPOT day-ahead auctions based on production forecasts, marginal cost curves and portfolio position, with cut-off deadline management.

activities.name: Intraday Power Trading
activities.description: Continuous buying and selling of electricity on intraday continuous markets to correct forecast deviations, capture price spreads and rebalance the position closer to delivery.

activities.name: Power Purchase Agreement Management
activities.description: Negotiation, execution and lifecycle administration of corporate and utility PPAs, including volume nomination, indexation tracking, settlement reconciliation and contract amendment workflows.

activities.name: Renewable Energy Production Forecasting
activities.description: Generation of short- and medium-term output forecasts for wind, solar and hydro assets using numerical weather prediction feeds and statistical models to feed trading, dispatch and grid nomination processes.

activities.name: Guarantees of Origin Issuance & Trading
activities.description: Registration, issuance, transfer and cancellation of Guarantees of Origin certificates through national registries to evidence the renewable attributes of delivered energy and monetise green value.

activities.name: Grid Connection & Nomination Management
activities.description: Submission of physical and commercial schedules to transmission and distribution system operators, management of connection-point capacity, and coordination of redispatch and curtailment instructions.

activities.name: Preventive Maintenance Scheduling
activities.description: Planning and dispatch of calendar- and condition-based maintenance work orders for turbines, inverters, transformers and hydro equipment, balancing technician availability against production loss windows.

activities.name: Wind Turbine Blade Inspection & Repair
activities.description: Drone and rope-access inspection of rotor blades for erosion, lightning damage and delamination, followed by in-situ composite repair campaigns and structural integrity sign-off.

activities.name: SCADA-Based Condition Monitoring
activities.description: Continuous analysis of vibration, temperature and oil-debris telemetry from rotating equipment to detect incipient failures, trigger predictive maintenance alerts and extend asset life.

activities.name: Spare Parts & Inventory Management
activities.description: Stocking, warehousing and replenishment of critical spare components such as gearboxes, bearings and power electronics across regional depots to minimise mean-time-to-repair.

activities.name: Substation & Transformer Maintenance
activities.description: Inspection, oil testing, tap-changer servicing and protection-relay calibration of collector substations and grid step-up transformers to ensure safe and reliable power evacuation.

activities.name: Field Technician Dispatch & Work Order Closure
activities.description: Routing of mobile maintenance crews to remote sites, capture of completed work evidence, parts consumption logging and quality-controlled closure of corrective work orders.

activities.name: Energy Metering & Settlement
activities.description: Collection and validation of revenue-grade meter data from generation points, reconciliation against market positions, and calculation of settlement amounts with counterparties and system operators.

activities.name: Customer Invoicing & Revenue Billing
activities.description: Generation of periodic invoices to corporate offtakers, retail customers and aggregators, applying tariffs, indexation and pass-through charges, and managing credit notes and adjustments.

activities.name: Accounts Receivable & Collections
activities.description: Monitoring of outstanding customer balances, dunning of overdue invoices, dispute resolution and management of bad-debt provisioning across the offtake portfolio.

activities.name: Procurement of Turbines & Major Equipment
activities.description: Sourcing, tendering and contracting of wind turbine generators, PV modules and balance-of-plant equipment, including technical evaluation, price negotiation and delivery-schedule commitment.

activities.name: Supplier Qualification & Vendor Onboarding
activities.description: Assessment of new suppliers against technical, financial, ESG and security criteria, due-diligence checks and registration of approved vendors into the master vendor database.

activities.name: Service Contractor Management
activities.description: Engagement and oversight of third-party O&M, civil works and balance-of-plant contractors, including SLA monitoring, performance reviews and contract renewal decisions.

activities.name: Land Lease & Landowner Relations
activities.description: Negotiation and administration of land lease and easement agreements with farmers, municipalities and forestry owners, including rent payments, access rights and renewal coordination.

activities.name: Recruitment & Talent Acquisition
activities.description: Sourcing, interviewing and hiring of engineering, trading and corporate staff, managing job requisitions, candidate pipelines and employment offers across European locations.

activities.name: Payroll & Compensation Administration
activities.description: Processing of monthly payroll across multiple jurisdictions, application of collective-agreement rates, benefits, social charges and tax withholding, and management of expense reimbursements.

activities.name: Employee Training & Competence Management
activities.description: Planning and delivery of safety, technical and compliance training, tracking of certifications and licences, and maintenance of competence matrices for operational roles.

activities.name: Workforce Health, Safety & Wellbeing
activities.description: Administration of occupational health programmes, fitness-for-work assessments, incident support and wellbeing initiatives for field and office personnel.

activities.name: IT Service Desk & End-User Support
activities.description: First- and second-line support for corporate IT, handling incidents and service requests for laptops, collaboration tools, identity access and business applications via a ticketing system.

activities.name: Corporate Network & Infrastructure Operations
activities.description: Operation of the enterprise WAN/LAN, data centres, cloud tenancy and connectivity to remote sites, including capacity planning, patching and availability monitoring.

activities.name: Cybersecurity Monitoring & Incident Response
activities.description: 24/7 detection, triage and response to security events across IT and OT environments through the SOC, including threat hunting, containment and post-incident review under NIS2 obligations.

activities.name: Identity & Access Management
activities.description: Provisioning, review and de-provisioning of user identities and privileged accounts, enforcement of least-privilege, multi-factor authentication and periodic access recertification.

activities.name: Enterprise Backup & Disaster Recovery
activities.description: Scheduled backup of corporate and operational data, replication to secondary sites and periodic restoration testing to meet recovery-time and recovery-point objectives.

activities.name: OT Control Room Operations
activities.description: Round-the-clock supervision of the centralised SCADA/EMS control room, monitoring plant status, alarms and grid conditions and coordinating operator responses to abnormal events.

activities.name: OT Network Security & Segmentation
activities.description: Hardening, segmentation and monitoring of the industrial control network connecting turbines, inverters and substation RTUs, enforcing IEC 62443 zone-and-conduit boundaries between IT and OT.

activities.name: Remote Asset Telemetry & Data Acquisition
activities.description: Secure collection, time-stamping and aggregation of operational telemetry from geographically dispersed generation assets into the central historian and analytics platform.

activities.name: Industrial Firmware & Patch Management
activities.description: Controlled testing, scheduling and deployment of firmware and security patches to PLCs, inverters and SCADA components with rollback planning to avoid production disruption.

activities.name: Plant Performance Engineering & Optimisation
activities.description: Analysis of asset performance ratios, availability and energy yield to identify underperformance, recommend setting changes and quantify the impact of optimisation interventions.

activities.name: Repowering & Lifetime Extension Studies
activities.description: Technical and financial evaluation of replacing ageing wind and solar assets with higher-capacity equipment or extending operational life beyond original design horizons.

activities.name: Energy Storage R&D and Hybridisation
activities.description: Research and pilot deployment of battery storage co-located with generation assets, including control-strategy development, degradation modelling and grid-service business cases.

activities.name: Green Hydrogen Pilot Project Development
activities.description: Exploratory development of electrolyser-based hydrogen production powered by surplus renewable generation, covering feasibility, technology selection and off-taker engagement.

activities.name: Grid-Forming Inverter Technology Trials
activities.description: Testing of advanced inverter control modes that provide synthetic inertia and black-start capability to support weak-grid integration of renewable plants.

activities.name: Regulatory & Statutory Reporting
activities.description: Preparation and submission of mandatory reports to energy regulators, the CRE and ACER, including REMIT transaction reporting, production declarations and market-participation disclosures.

activities.name: NIS2 Compliance & Incident Notification
activities.description: Maintenance of the NIS2 cybersecurity governance baseline and timely notification of significant incidents to the national CSIRT and competent authority within regulatory deadlines.

activities.name: GDPR Data Protection Operations
activities.description: Handling of personal-data processing records, data-subject access requests, breach assessment and notification, and privacy-impact assessments under the supervision of the DPO.

activities.name: Environmental & Biodiversity Monitoring
activities.description: Ongoing monitoring of avian, bat and aquatic impacts at generation sites, compliance with permit conditions and reporting to environmental authorities.

activities.name: ISO 27001 ISMS Governance
activities.description: Operation of the information security management system, including risk treatment, statement-of-applicability maintenance, internal audits and management-review cycles.

activities.name: ESG & Sustainability Reporting
activities.description: Collection of environmental, social and governance metrics and preparation of CSRD-aligned sustainability disclosures and carbon-footprint reporting for stakeholders.

activities.name: Customer Support & Offtaker Helpdesk
activities.description: Handling of enquiries, contract questions and service requests from corporate PPA offtakers and aggregator partners through a dedicated relationship desk.

activities.name: Connection Request & Onboarding for New Offtakers
activities.description: Management of the onboarding journey for new corporate energy buyers, from initial enquiry through contract setup, metering alignment and go-live coordination.

activities.name: Complaint & Dispute Handling
activities.description: Logging, investigation and resolution of customer and counterparty complaints regarding billing, delivery or service quality, with escalation and root-cause tracking.

activities.name: Treasury & Cash Flow Management
activities.description: Management of corporate liquidity, bank relationships, intra-group funding and short-term cash positioning to support project investment and operating needs.

activities.name: Energy Commodity Risk Hedging
activities.description: Execution of forward and futures hedging strategies to manage exposure to power-price, gas-price and balancing-cost volatility within board-approved risk limits.

activities.name: Project Finance & Investment Appraisal
activities.description: Financial modelling, due diligence and structuring of debt and equity for new renewable development projects, including lender liaison and investment-committee submissions.

activities.name: Insurance & Claims Management
activities.description: Placement and renewal of property, business-interruption and liability insurance for the asset fleet and handling of claims following equipment failures or weather events.

activities.name: Legal & Contract Lifecycle Management
activities.description: Drafting, review, negotiation and repository management of commercial, employment and supplier contracts, plus oversight of litigation and regulatory legal matters.

activities.name: Internal Audit & Controls Assurance
activities.description: Independent, risk-based audits of operational, financial and IT processes to verify control effectiveness, report findings to the audit committee and track remediation actions.

indicators.name: Critical vulnerability remediation latency (SCADA/OT)

indicators.name: EDR agent coverage on managed endpoints

indicators.name: Multi-factor authentication enrolment for privileged accounts

indicators.name: Privileged access reviews completed on time

indicators.name: Phishing-reported email rate

indicators.name: Security incidents reported to authorities within NIS2 window

indicators.name: Firmware currency of field IoT/RTU devices

indicators.name: Privileged session recording coverage

indicators.name: Annual penetration test findings remediated

indicators.name: Business continuity exercise participation
indicators.description: Proportion of designated crisis and recovery team members who took part in the most recent BCP/DR tabletop or live exercise.

indicators.name: Records of processing activities kept current

indicators.name: Asset inventory accuracy

indicators.name: Privileged accounts with stale passwords

indicators.name: Recovery time objective met in DR tests

indicators.name: Physical access badge recertification

indicators.name: Code repositories with secret scanning enabled
indicators.description: Proportion of active source repositories with automated secret detection enabled in the CI/CD pipeline.

indicators.name: Open critical SOC alerts beyond SLA

indicators.name: Tabletop-tested incident response playbooks

indicators.name: Cloud workload misconfiguration findings open

indicators.name: Management review actions completed

essential_assets.name: Wind Turbine SCADA Telemetry Stream
essential_assets.description: Real-time operational telemetry (rotor speed, pitch angle, gearbox temperature, power output) collected from the onshore wind fleet SCADA network. Critical for availability and integrity under NIS2 as it drives turbine control decisions.

essential_assets.name: Solar PV Plant Performance & Yield Database
essential_assets.description: Historical and live generation, irradiance, and inverter performance records for the solar portfolio, used for forecasting, warranty claims, and contractual availability reporting.

essential_assets.name: Hydroelectric Dam Flow & Reservoir Level Records
essential_assets.description: Water inflow, reservoir level, and turbine discharge measurements for run-of-river and reservoir hydro plants. Safety-critical data feeding both generation optimisation and regulatory dam-safety obligations.

essential_assets.name: Energy Trading & Power Purchase Agreement Process
essential_assets.description: Business process covering day-ahead and intraday electricity market bidding, balancing responsibility, and execution of PPAs with offtakers. High confidentiality and integrity needs given market-sensitive positions.

essential_assets.name: Grid Balancing & Dispatch Coordination Process
essential_assets.description: Operational process coordinating generation dispatch with the French and EU TSO/DSO balancing instructions, redispatch orders, and curtailment signals. Availability-critical and in NIS2 scope as an electricity-sector essential service.

essential_assets.name: Renewable Energy Guarantees of Origin Registry
essential_assets.description: Records of issued, transferred, and cancelled Guarantees of Origin certificates proving the renewable source of generated electricity, integrity-critical to prevent double counting and fraud.

essential_assets.name: Predictive Maintenance Process for Wind Fleet
essential_assets.description: Condition-based maintenance process using vibration and oil-analysis data to schedule turbine interventions, balancing availability, safety, and supplier coordination.

essential_assets.name: Customer & Offtaker Contract Repository
essential_assets.description: Repository of signed PPAs, B2B energy supply contracts, and counterparty terms including named signatories and commercial contacts. Contains personal data of corporate representatives.

essential_assets.name: Employee Personnel & HR Records
essential_assets.description: Master HR records covering contracts, payroll references, performance reviews, and emergency contacts for Voltara staff. High confidentiality and GDPR-sensitive employee personal data.

essential_assets.name: Field Technician Dispatch & Work Order Process
essential_assets.description: Process for planning, assigning, and tracking on-site maintenance interventions across wind, solar, and hydro sites, including lone-worker safety scheduling.

essential_assets.name: Plant Cybersecurity Incident Response Process
essential_assets.description: Process for detecting, triaging, containing, and reporting OT/IT security incidents, including the NIS2 24-hour early-warning and 72-hour notification obligations to the national CSIRT.

essential_assets.name: GDPR Data Subject Request Handling Process
essential_assets.description: Process for receiving, verifying, and fulfilling access, rectification, and erasure requests from employees, contractors, and contacts, with regulatory deadlines and identity verification handling personal data.

essential_assets.name: Remote Operations Control Centre Workflow
essential_assets.description: 24/7 control-room process for remotely monitoring and operating the renewable fleet from Lyon, including shift handover, alarm acknowledgement, and escalation. Availability-critical NIS2 service.

essential_assets.name: Wind Resource Assessment & Site Prospecting Data
essential_assets.description: Meteorological mast measurements, LiDAR campaigns, and wind-flow models for prospective development sites. Commercially sensitive and confidentiality-critical for competitive land acquisition.

essential_assets.name: Land Lease & Landowner Agreement Files
essential_assets.description: Land lease contracts, easements, and right-of-way agreements with private landowners and farmers hosting turbines and panels, containing names, cadastral references, and bank details for lease payments.

essential_assets.name: Grid Connection & Interconnection Permit Records
essential_assets.description: Connection agreements, capacity allocation studies, and interconnection permits with TSOs and DSOs for each plant. Integrity-critical for regulatory compliance and dispatch rights.

essential_assets.name: Project Development & Permitting Process
essential_assets.description: End-to-end process for advancing greenfield projects through environmental impact assessment, public consultation, and building permits, handling stakeholder and objector personal data.

essential_assets.name: Environmental Monitoring & Biodiversity Data
essential_assets.description: Bat and avian mortality logs, noise measurements, and protected-species monitoring records required by environmental permits for wind and hydro sites.

essential_assets.name: Turbine OEM Warranty & Service Records
essential_assets.description: Warranty terms, service bulletins, and component replacement history per turbine supplier, integrity-critical for claims and remaining-useful-life decisions.

essential_assets.name: Financial Consolidation & Reporting Process
essential_assets.description: Process for consolidating multi-entity financials, project SPV accounts, and group reporting under IFRS, with high integrity and confidentiality requirements.

essential_assets.name: Project Finance & Debt Covenant Records
essential_assets.description: Loan agreements, lender covenant tests, and debt service coverage calculations for project-financed plants. Highly confidential and integrity-critical for lender relations.

essential_assets.name: Procurement & Supplier Onboarding Process
essential_assets.description: Process for qualifying, contracting, and onboarding equipment vendors and service providers, including due diligence and supplier contact personal data.

essential_assets.name: Spare Parts Inventory & Logistics Data
essential_assets.description: Stock levels, reorder thresholds, and warehouse locations for critical turbine and inverter spares across regional depots, availability-critical for fast repair response.

essential_assets.name: Health, Safety & Environment Incident Register
essential_assets.description: Records of workplace accidents, near-misses, and safety observations including injured-worker details. GDPR-sensitive special-category health data of employees and contractors.

essential_assets.name: Working-at-Height & Confined-Space Permit Process
essential_assets.description: Safety process governing permits-to-work for nacelle access, tower climbing, and dam intake inspections, integrity-critical to prevent fatalities.

essential_assets.name: OT Network Access & Remote Connection Process
essential_assets.description: Process governing how engineers and OEM vendors obtain remote access to plant control systems, including identity verification and session logging of named users.

essential_assets.name: Identity & Access Management Directory
essential_assets.description: Central directory of user accounts, roles, group memberships, and authentication credentials for corporate and OT systems. Confidentiality-critical and contains employee personal data.

essential_assets.name: Energy Production Forecasting Models
essential_assets.description: Statistical and machine-learning models combining weather forecasts with plant characteristics to predict generation for market bidding. Confidential intellectual property and integrity-critical for trading.

essential_assets.name: Regulatory Reporting & Compliance Filing Process
essential_assets.description: Process for preparing and submitting filings to the CRE, ADEME, and EU regulators, covering subsidy claims, capacity declarations, and NIS2 conformity attestations.

essential_assets.name: Carbon Accounting & ESG Disclosure Data
essential_assets.description: Scope 1/2/3 emissions, lifecycle assessment results, and CSRD-aligned sustainability metrics used for investor and regulatory ESG reporting.

essential_assets.name: Insurance & Claims Management Process
essential_assets.description: Process for placing property, liability, and business-interruption cover and managing claims after storms or equipment failure, handling loss adjuster and claimant personal data.

essential_assets.name: Dam Safety Surveillance & Inspection Process
essential_assets.description: Regulated process for periodic geotechnical inspection, instrumentation review, and emergency action planning for hydro dams. Safety-critical with strict regulatory oversight.

essential_assets.name: Plant Engineering Drawings & As-Built Documentation

threats.name: Compromise of cloud SCADA historian provider
threats.description: Breach at the third-party SaaS hosting the operational data historian, exposing aggregated generation telemetry across all sites.

threats.name: Malicious firmware in imported solar inverters
threats.description: Backdoored inverter controllers from a low-cost supplier allowing remote curtailment or shutdown of PV arrays.

threats.name: Credential stuffing against employee SSO portal
threats.description: Automated reuse of leaked passwords against the single sign-on login to hijack staff accounts lacking strong MFA.

threats.name: Watering-hole attack on renewable-industry association site
threats.description: Compromise of a sector association website frequented by Voltara staff, serving exploits to infect engineering workstations.

threats.name: SIM-swap takeover of an executive's mobile number
threats.description: Fraudulent number porting to intercept SMS one-time codes and reset accounts belonging to a senior leader.

threats.name: Counterfeit network hardware introduced via grey-market reseller
threats.description: Non-genuine switches or routers procured through an unauthorized reseller, carrying hidden implants or unsupported firmware.

threats.name: Theft of grid-balancing trading strategy by competitor
threats.description: Industrial espionage targeting Voltara's intraday energy-trading and balancing algorithms to gain market advantage.

threats.name: Exploited misconfigured public cloud storage bucket
threats.description: Attacker discovering an internet-exposed object-storage bucket containing engineering drawings and contracts.

threats.name: Disgruntled SCADA engineer plants logic bomb
threats.description: A frustrated control engineer embeds delayed malicious logic in turbine controllers to trigger an outage after departure.

threats.name: Departing employee exfiltrates project pipeline data
threats.description: An outgoing developer copies confidential site-development and bid data to personal storage before leaving the company.

threats.name: Accidental misconfiguration of firewall exposes OT network
threats.description: An administrator's erroneous rule change inadvertently exposes the operational technology segment to the corporate LAN.

threats.name: Privileged admin abuses access to alter generation records
threats.description: An IT administrator manipulates production and metering data to conceal an operational incident, undermining data integrity.

threats.name: Employee falls for phishing and discloses credentials
threats.description: A staff member enters their password on a spoofed login page, handing access to an external attacker.

threats.name: Shadow IT cloud app stores customer data unsanctioned
threats.description: A team adopts an unapproved SaaS tool processing customer personal data outside GDPR governance and contracts.

threats.name: Unauthorized USB device introduces malware to a workstation
threats.description: An employee connects an infected personal USB drive to an engineering PC, bypassing endpoint controls.

threats.name: Improper offboarding leaves active accounts for ex-staff
threats.description: Failure to revoke accounts and badges after termination leaves valid access paths for former employees.

threats.name: Contractor mishandles confidential grid connection documents
threats.description: An external contractor emails sensitive grid-interconnection studies to a personal account, risking leakage.

threats.name: Negligent disposal of decommissioned drives with data
threats.description: Retired servers and laptops scrapped without secure wiping, leaving recoverable confidential and personal data.

threats.name: Excessive access rights from role accumulation
threats.description: Long-tenured employees accumulate permissions across moves, creating toxic combinations and least-privilege violations.

threats.name: Operator error during turbine remote control causes trip
threats.description: A control-room operator issues an incorrect command remotely, causing an unintended shutdown of a wind cluster.

threats.name: Weak passwords reused across operational systems
threats.description: Staff reuse simple passwords on multiple OT and IT systems, easing lateral movement after a single compromise.

threats.name: Sensitive data emailed to wrong external recipient
threats.description: An employee mistakenly sends a spreadsheet of personal or contractual data to an incorrect address, a GDPR breach.

threats.name: Failure to apply security patch within SLA window
threats.description: Operations team delays critical patching of internet-facing systems, leaving a known vulnerability exploitable.

threats.name: Unencrypted laptop with project data lost in transit
threats.description: A field engineer loses an unencrypted laptop containing site survey and customer data during travel.

threats.name: Hardware failure of primary SCADA control server
threats.description: Unexpected failure of the main control server hardware interrupts monitoring and dispatch of generation assets.

threats.name: Storage array corruption damages historian data
threats.description: Latent bit-rot or controller fault corrupts the long-term generation and metering data archive.

threats.name: Expired TLS certificate breaks customer portal access
threats.description: An unrenewed certificate causes browser errors and blocks customer logins to the energy portal.

threats.name: Backup restoration failure during recovery test
threats.description: Backups prove incomplete or unrestorable when needed, undermining recovery from a data-loss event.

threats.name: Network segmentation collapse between IT and OT
threats.description: Failure of the firewall or VLAN segmentation merges corporate and control networks, broadening attack surface.

threats.name: Software bug in dispatch optimization causes overgeneration
threats.description: A defect in the energy-dispatch algorithm miscalculates setpoints, risking grid imbalance penalties.

threats.name: Telemetry latency from degraded SCADA telecom link
threats.description: Deterioration of the WAN link to remote sites delays critical telemetry and alarms beyond safe thresholds.

threats.name: Single points of failure in core network switching
threats.description: Non-redundant core switch creates a chokepoint whose failure isolates the data center from operations.

threats.name: Memory exhaustion crashes the monitoring platform
threats.description: A resource leak in the monitoring application causes repeated crashes and loss of situational awareness.

threats.name: Misbehaving firmware update bricks field RTUs
threats.description: A faulty automatic firmware push renders remote terminal units at substations unresponsive.

threats.name: Power supply failure in primary data center
threats.description: UPS and generator transfer failure during a utility outage shuts down the main data center hosting core systems.

threats.name: Database replication lag causes inconsistent reporting
threats.description: Replication delays between primary and reporting databases produce stale figures in compliance dashboards.

threats.name: Lightning strike damages substation control electronics
threats.description: A direct or induced lightning surge destroys protection relays and control gear at an exposed substation.

threats.name: Severe storm topples turbines and severs grid links
threats.description: Extreme wind events exceeding design limits damage turbine structures and overhead connection lines.

threats.name: River flooding inundates a hydro plant control room
threats.description: Seasonal flooding of the river basin submerges hydroelectric control equipment, causing extended downtime.

threats.name: Wildfire threatens a ground-mounted solar farm
threats.description: Regional wildfire approaching a PV site damages panels and cabling and forces protective shutdown.

threats.name: Heatwave overheats data center cooling capacity
threats.description: Prolonged extreme heat overwhelms cooling, forcing thermal throttling or shutdown of critical servers.

threats.name: Ice and snow loading disrupts wind generation
threats.description: Heavy icing on blades and sensors degrades output and damages equipment at high-altitude wind sites.

vulnerabilities.name: Unpatched Modbus/TCP firmware on wind turbine SCADA controllers
vulnerabilities.description: Programmable logic controllers governing the Pellafol onshore wind farm run a vendor firmware release that is two major versions behind, missing patches for known authentication-bypass flaws in the Modbus/TCP stack. Vendor maintenance windows are limited to two outage slots per year, so fixes lag by months while the OT network remains exposed to lateral movement from the corporate VPN.

vulnerabilities.name: Flat OT/IT network with no segmentation at the Roussillon solar park
vulnerabilities.description: The Roussillon photovoltaic site connects inverter management interfaces, the local engineering workstation and office laptops to a single VLAN with no firewall between the operational technology and corporate zones. A compromise of any office endpoint grants direct routable access to inverter controllers and the site RTU, violating IEC 62443 zone-and-conduit expectations.

vulnerabilities.name: Shared local administrator password reused across substation HMIs
vulnerabilities.description: All human-machine interface panels at grid-connection substations share an identical local administrator credential set during commissioning by the integrator. The password has never been rotated, is documented in a commissioning spreadsheet on a shared drive, and is known to several former contractors, enabling unauthenticated privileged access if any single panel is reached.

vulnerabilities.name: Internet-exposed remote desktop on the hydro plant maintenance jump host
vulnerabilities.description: A Windows jump host used for vendor maintenance of the Drôme run-of-river hydro turbines exposes RDP directly to the internet on the standard port behind only a NAT rule. There is no VPN, no IP allow-listing and no multi-factor authentication, leaving it subject to credential-stuffing and exploitation of RDP vulnerabilities.

vulnerabilities.name: Default vendor credentials on solar inverter cloud management portal
vulnerabilities.description: Several string inverters at the Camargue and Béziers arrays were registered in the manufacturer's cloud monitoring portal using factory default installer credentials that were never changed. The portal allows remote curtailment and firmware push, so the unchanged defaults could let an attacker manipulate generation or brick devices.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing multi-factor authentication on the corporate VPN
vulnerabilities.description: Remote access to the Voltara corporate network via the SSL VPN relies on single-factor username and password only. Field engineers and a third-party SOC connect through this VPN, so a single phished or leaked credential provides full internal network reach with no second factor to contain the breach.

vulnerabilities.name: End-of-life Windows Server 2012 R2 hosting the energy management system
vulnerabilities.description: The central energy management and dispatch application runs on a Windows Server 2012 R2 instance that has reached end of extended support and no longer receives security updates. The server cannot be upgraded in place because the dispatch software is incompatible with newer OS releases, leaving an unsupported critical system in production.

vulnerabilities.name: Unencrypted Modbus telemetry between turbines and central SCADA
vulnerabilities.description: Telemetry and control traffic between wind turbine controllers and the central SCADA historian traverses leased fibre and microwave links in cleartext Modbus with no transport encryption or message authentication. An adversary with link access could read operational data or inject forged setpoint commands undetected.

vulnerabilities.name: Overly permissive Azure storage account hosting GDPR personnel data
vulnerabilities.description: An Azure Blob storage account holding scanned employee contracts and ID documents was provisioned with public network access enabled and a broad shared-access signature with no expiry. The misconfiguration risks exposure of special-category and identifying personal data, a notifiable breach under GDPR Articles 33 and 34.
